findings.title: Suspected Pre-Departure Data Exfiltration
findings.description: James King (sales) showed a 40x spike in data downloads from the customer PII datastore in the 14 days prior to simulation end. Activity concentrated in evening hours (5pm-10pm), outside normal work pattern.
detection_signals: Volume anomaly: 40x baseline download rate
Time anomaly: activity outside normal work hours
Datastore sensitivity: PII classification
Behavioral change: sustained over 14-day period
findings.mitre_technique: T1537 - Transfer Data to Cloud Account
findings.recommended_action: Suspend account, initiate DLP review, preserve logs for forensics

findings.title: Active Access by Departed Employee
findings.description: Rachel Rivera departed on 2024-02-05T00:00:00 but their account remains active. 4 access events detected after departure, targeting sensitive datastores including PII and employee records.
detection_signals: Account active past departure date
Access 55 days post-departure
No offboarding record in identity system
MFA disabled — account potentially compromised
findings.recommended_action: Immediately disable account, audit all post-departure activity, involve HR and Legal

findings.title: Possible Credential Compromise — Foreign IP Access to Sensitive Data
findings.description: William Taylor (data_science) accessed critical datastores from IP 103.21.244.115 (foreign range) between 1am-5am over 4 days. Normal access pattern is standard from 192.168.1.203.
detection_signals: IP deviation: normal 192.168.1.203 vs suspicious 103.21.244.115
Time anomaly: 1am-5am activity, outside all work patterns
Geolocation: foreign IP range, not VPN
Data sensitivity: PHI and PCI datastores accessed
No travel notice logged in HR system
findings.recommended_action: Force password reset, revoke active sessions, enable step-up MFA, review accessed records

findings.title: Service Account Actively Using Excess Permissions
findings.description: ETL pipeline service account (svc-etl-pipeline) has permissions to 4 datastores but only requires 2 for its stated function. Over 90 days, it made 28 accesses to patient records (PHI) and billing database (PCI) — datastores with no documented ETL requirement.
detection_signals: Service account accessing PHI datastore with no documented requirement
Service account accessing PCI datastore with no documented requirement
Consistent pattern over 90 days — not one-time
No change ticket or approval found for these access patterns
Credentials not rotated in 340 days
findings.recommended_action: Scope service account to minimum required permissions, rotate credentials, audit all historical access

findings.title: PII Data Found in Unclassified Development Bucket
findings.description: Timothy Martin copied 450MB from the customer PII datastore to the dev scratch bucket on 2024-01-16. The dev bucket has no encryption enforcement, no DLP controls, and broader access than production. 6 subsequent accesses by other engineers detected.
detection_signals: Large CopyObject from PII-classified to INTERNAL-classified bucket
Classification mismatch: PII data in non-PII bucket
Dev bucket lacks encryption at rest enforcement
Dev bucket accessible to all engineers (18 users)
Data exposed for 75 days without remediation
compliance_impact: GDPR Art.25 - Data Protection by Design
findings.recommended_action: Delete copied data immediately, add bucket policy to prevent cross-classification copies, classify dev bucket correctly
